properties.name: modal
properties.description: Exposes the internal modal utility that controls focus trapping. To temporarily disable focus trapping and allow third-party modals spawned from an active modal, call `modal.activateExternal()` when the third-party modal opens. Upon closing, call `modal.deactivateExternal()` to restore focus trapping.

properties.name: open
properties.description:
Indicates whether or not the dialog is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the dialog, or you can
use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the dialog's open state.

properties.name: label
properties.description:
The dialog's label as displayed in the header. You should always include a relevant label even when using
`no-header`, as it is required for proper accessibility. If you need to display HTML, use the `label` slot instead.

properties.name: noHeader
properties.description:
Disables the header. This will also remove the default close button, so please ensure you provide an easy,
accessible way for users to dismiss the dialog.

events.name: x-after-show
events.description: Emitted after the dialog opens and all animations are complete.

events.name: x-after-hide
events.description: Emitted after the dialog closes and all animations are complete.

events.name: x-initial-focus
events.description: Emitted when the dialog opens and is ready to receive focus. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will prevent focusing and allow you to set it on a different element, such as an input.

events.name: x-request-close
events.description: Emitted when the user attempts to close the dialog by clicking the close button, clicking the overlay, or pressing escape. Calling `event.preventDefault()` will keep the dialog open. Avoid using this unless closing the dialog will result in destructive behavior such as data loss.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The dialog's label. Alternatively, you can use the `label` attribute.

slots.name: header-actions
slots.description: Optional actions to add to the header. Works best with `<x-icon-button>`.

slots.name: footer
slots.description: The dialog's footer, usually one or more buttons representing various options.

cssProperties.name: --width
cssProperties.description: The preferred width of the dialog. Note that the dialog will shrink to accommodate smaller screens.

cssProperties.name: --header-spacing
cssProperties.description: The amount of padding to use for the header.

cssProperties.name: --body-spacing
cssProperties.description: The amount of padding to use for the body.

cssProperties.name: --footer-spacing
cssProperties.description: The amount of padding to use for the footer.

cssParts.name: overlay
cssParts.description: The overlay that covers the screen behind the dialog.

cssParts.name: panel
cssParts.description: The dialog's panel (where the dialog and its content are rendered).

cssParts.name: header
cssParts.description: The dialog's header. This element wraps the title and header actions.

cssParts.name: header-actions
cssParts.description: Optional actions to add to the header. Works best with `<x-icon-button>`.
